faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What are the check-in and check-out times?
faq.a1: Check-in is from 14:00 onwards, and check-out is by 11:00 at the latest. You can contact us in advance for flexible times.
faq.a2: The property pool is open from June to November. There is an adult and a children's pool.
faq.a3: Yes, free open-air parking is available within the property.
faq.q4: Is there a crib or high chair?
faq.a4: Yes, a baby crib and high chair are provided free of charge upon request.
faq.q6: How do I get to the beach?
faq.a6: The beach is approximately a 15-20 minute walk (~2 km). You can reach Kemer beach in about 10 minutes by car.
faq.a8: Airport transfer can be arranged upon request. Contact us via WhatsApp for details and pricing.

attractions.title: Places to Visit
attractions.subtitle: Discover the natural and historical beauties just minutes away from Palmiye Home.
attractions.canyon_short: A natural wonder with waterfalls and pine forests
attractions.canyon_desc: One of Kemer's most popular attractions, Göynük Canyon is located on the Lycian Way within the Beydağları Coastal National Park. This natural wonder features stunning scenery, rich vegetation, and waterfalls. The area also contains artificial ponds, streams, and natural pools. Ranked among the world's top 10 trekking routes, it's an essential destination for nature lovers. You can swim in its ice-blue waters, enjoy camping facilities, and create an unforgettable holiday itinerary.
attractions.phaselis_short: Ancient port city where you can swim among ruins
attractions.phaselis_desc: Founded by Rhodians in the 7th century BC, this city sits on a peninsula extending into the Mediterranean. For years it served as one of the most important ports on Lycia's eastern coast, boasting four harbors. The surviving ruins mostly date from Roman and Byzantine periods, including a main avenue connecting north and south harbors, a square, theater, temple remnants, and three agoras.
attractions.olympos_short: Ancient ruins, forest trails and a stunning beach
attractions.olympos_desc: Another renowned port city after Phaselis, Olympos features ruins from the Roman, Byzantine, and Hellenistic periods. The city stretches east-west, situated at the river mouth and connected by a bridge — one pillar still stands today. Follow the stream path through the ancient city to reach the beautiful Olympos Beach on foot.
attractions.tahtali_short: Ride up to 2,365m for breathtaking panoramic views
attractions.tahtali_desc: Standing at 2,365 meters, Tahtalı is one of the tallest mountains in the region. The summit is accessible by cable car from Olympos, offering spectacular nature views along the way. This cable car holds the title of Europe's longest and the world's second longest.
attractions.cirali_desc: Known as the "Eternal Flame of Olympos," this remarkable site features naturally burning rocks fueled by methane gas seeping from the earth. Referenced in Homer's texts and mythologized over centuries, the fires have been considered sacred. These stones have been burning continuously for over 2,500 years.
attractions.adrasan_short: Crystal-clear shallow waters, fine sand beach
attractions.adrasan_desc: One of the most popular beaches in the area with pristine waters, Adrasan Bay is about an hour from Kemer. With a 4,000-year history, this location is surrounded by orange and pine trees. The bay's most distinctive feature is its shallow, stone-free, fine-sand beach with calm waters.
attractions.paris2_short: One of the world's top 100 diving spots
attractions.paris2_desc: Listed among the world's 100 most remarkable diving spots, Paris II is a French ship that sank in Kemer waters during World War I. Resting 33 meters offshore, the wreck still contains six anti-aircraft guns and two torpedo tubes, making it a fascinating underwater museum.
attractions.alacasu_short: A hidden paradise with turquoise waters
attractions.alacasu_desc: Considered one of Kemer's hidden gems, this cove is about 90 minutes from the center. With its 175-meter beach, trekking trails, crystal-blue swimming waters, and camping and picnic areas, it's an ideal escape from the city.
attractions.beldibi_short: Prehistoric cave art dating back millennia
attractions.beldibi_desc: Located about 20 kilometers from Kemer, this cave sits where the sea meets the rocks. Its walls feature animal and human figures. Research has uncovered artifacts from the Neolithic, Paleolithic, and Mesolithic eras.
attractions.idyros_short: Byzantine-era geometric mosaics in Kemer center
attractions.idyros_desc: Uncovered during excavations in 1976, this ancient city harbors artifacts from the Byzantine period. The most distinctive find is a geometric-patterned mosaic floor. Located right in Kemer's town center.
